items.headline: X-energy raises over $1 billion in largest nuclear IPO on record
items.body: Small modular reactor company X-energy completed public offering on April 24 with Amazon backing. Energy agency head warned Europe has six weeks of jet fuel left as Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights through 2026.

items.headline: Tanzania reports 518 killed in election violence, far below opposition estimates
items.body: Government commission documented deaths from last year's election, mostly from gunshot wounds. Commission head called the toll not final or conclusive.

items.headline: Peru defense and foreign ministers resign over fighter jet purchase dispute
items.body: Cabinet members quit April 22 after interim president deferred F-16 decision from Lockheed Martin. Economy ministry transferred $462 million initial payment despite president's declared suspension.

items.headline: Japan warns of heightened earthquake risk after 7.4 magnitude strike
items.body: Earthquake on April 20 triggered tsunami warnings for waves up to three meters. Officials placed 182 towns on alert with 1% chance of magnitude 8 quake in next week.

es.headline: Barcelona confirms Lamine Yamal's domestic season ended by injury sustained against Celta Vigo
es.body: The forward suffered the injury on April 22 and will miss Barcelona's title run. Spain faces uncertainty over his availability for World Cup opener on June 15.

no.headline: Norway to ban social media for children under 16 starting 2027
no.body: Government announced bill making accounts illegal before January first of year users turn sixteen. Tech companies must verify age under strictest European child digital safety framework.

uk.headline: Prime Minister Starmer apologizes for Mandelson appointment after security vetting override revealed
uk.body: Starmer told Parliament on April 20 he would not have appointed the US Ambassador had he known vetting officials recommended denial in January 2025. Foreign Office permanent secretary was sacked after testimony revealed Downing Street pressured clearance despite Epstein intelligence concerns.

nl.headline: Dutch intelligence warns of greatest security threat in 80 years from Russia and China
nl.body: AIVD released annual report Thursday citing Russian cyberattacks on police and Chinese targeting of quantum technology. Agency head said military conflict between Russia and West is no longer unthinkable.
